<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/717#issuecomment-4363440994" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**9 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Yeah, I agree with the above comment that they're similar.

From my understanding, Separation of Concerns is more like a high level principle. It involves splitting a system into modular parts. Like with an example `Bank` simulation system, we split it into things like a `Queue`, a `Customer`, a `Bank Teller`, `Money` etc. We are only interested in making sure code is split such that they don't overlap.

Single Responsibility Principle is more bottom-up, making sure that any class will only be affected by changes related to its responsibility and nothing else. It's more concrete because we can say something like a `Customer` shouldn't be affected by modifications to `Money` and its responsibilities. A `Customer` should only be affected by modifications to the behaviour they exhibit like **depositing/withdrawing** `Money`, but not be affected by how `Money` behaves with the rest of the system.
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/762#issuecomment-4364215151" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**10 :fas-comment:**

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/769#issuecomment-4366398087" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**11 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I think using the diamond-shaped merge nodes are more correct.

It shows that there is an alternate flow that can also reach this point in the activity diagram, and from then they are follow the same flow. Thus, I feel that they highlight that the alternate flows come from some other branch, which helps make it more explicit. 

As mentioned in the notes, omitting the merge nodes is allowed, but only if it doesn't introduce ambiguities, so your mileage may vary

<img width="841" height="693" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9e5d2dee-eb6f-4b7a-8e14-57a1b47ba40d" />
</panel>

</panel>

### 72. CHIA..XUAN `@chiabingxuan` (10 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/254" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Length of methods for JUnit test classes**
</div>

Hello all, would like to ask a question on the length of methods for JUnit test classes.

Currently I have a few methods in `ParserTest` which have been flagged as excessively long, for example:

'''java

    public void parse_correctFormat_success() throws Exception ``{``
        // todo provided in correct format
        assertEquals("add command: [T][ ] borrow book",
                Parser.parse("todo borrow book").toString());

        // deadline provided in correct format
        assertEquals("add command: [D][ ] do laundry (by: 1 Mar 2025, 11:00 am)",
                Parser.parse("deadline do laundry /by 1/3/2025 11:00").toString());

        // event provided in correct format
        assertEquals("add command: [E][ ] chess training "
                        + "(from: 1 Mar 2025, 3:00 pm to: 1 Mar 2025, 6:00 pm)",
                Parser.parse("event chess training /from 1/3/2025 15:00 "
                        + "/to 1/3/2025 18:00").toString());

        // list provided in correct format
        assertEquals("list command",
                Parser.parse("list").toString());

        // list provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("list command",
                Parser.parse("    list  ").toString());

        // remind provided in correct format
        assertEquals("remind command: 2",
                Parser.parse("remind 2").toString());

        // remind provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("remind command: 3",
                Parser.parse("    remind  3 ").toString());

        // bye provided in correct format
        assertEquals("bye command",
                Parser.parse("bye").toString());

        // bye provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("bye command",
                Parser.parse("    bye  ").toString());

        // mark provided in correct format
        assertEquals("mark command: 0",
                Parser.parse("mark 1").toString());

        // unmark provided in correct format
        assertEquals("unmark command: 2",
                Parser.parse("unmark 3").toString());

        // delete provided in correct format
        assertEquals("delete command: 1",
                Parser.parse("delete 2").toString());

        // find provided in correct format
        assertEquals("find command: running errands",
                Parser.parse("find running errands").toString());

        // find provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("find command: running errands",
                Parser.parse("find running errands   ").toString());

        // find provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("find command: running errands",
                Parser.parse("find      running errands").toString());

        // find provided in correct format with whitespace
        assertEquals("find command: running   errands",
                Parser.parse("find running   errands").toString());
    ``}``
'''

However, I am not sure whether it is worth writing individual methods for each of these `assertEquals()` calls, as I think the readability of the current code is still not impaired. May I receive some advice? Thank you!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/157" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: 💡Fix mouse scrolling issue in GUI**
</div>

Hello everyone, I would like to share a fix to an issue that I noticed, with regards to mouse scrolling in the GUI.

Previously, I had noticed that upon booting up the program, the chat history was unresponsive to scrolling with the mouse, even after we click on the pane. However, after manually scrolling with the scroll bar, we are then finally able to scroll with the mouse.

To solve this, we can make the following changes. Instead of having this method:

'''java
@FXML
public void initialize() ``{``
    scrollPane.vvalueProperty().bind(dialogContainer.heightProperty());
``}``
'''

we can instead add the following line to the end of the `handleUserInput()` method:

'''java
// autoscroll downwards after the new message bubbles have been added
Platform.runLater(() -&gt; scrollPane.setVvalue(1.0));
'''

and the issue is solved.

In the new code, the adjustment of the vvalue is made after each user input, thus preserving the downwards autoscroll. However, I'm not too sure why the previous code led to the mouse scrolling problem, nor do I understand why the new code solves the issue. Would anyone be able to offer an explanation? Thank you!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/133#issuecomment-3854401736" expanded>

<img width="610" height="1036" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6fb0d6f3-6484-4fc5-8141-a52d642c1954" />

Hello everyone, would like to share my GUI. Here are some pointers that I have implemented:

- Use polymorphism to distinguish between different kinds of messages that can be produced by the chatbot: `SuccessMessage`, `WarningMessage` and `ErrorMessage`, all inheriting from the base `Message` class. These messages will be formatted differently, according to its type. For example, `Message` is blue,  `SuccessMessage` is green, `WarningMessage` is yellow and `ErrorMessage` is red
- Display an initial `WarningMessage` if there is some issue during the loading of existing tasks
- Use different formats to parse dates in input and display dates in output
- Show informative `ErrorMessage`s. For example, the user is directed to a list of known commands, should an invalid command be issued
</panel>

Hello @ojunyi 

Thank you for your help, appreciate it!

With regards to the final issue that you raised, I suspect that the problem arises because the JAR file was placed in the `system32` folder (looking at the error message). Administrative privileges are required to read / write from this folder. Hence, the app could not initialise the task storage in this folder. 

To verify this on my end, I have tried placing the JAR file in my `system32` folder - indeed, I ran into the same issue that you described.

Would it be possible to try placing the JAR file in a newly created folder on the desktop? Thank you!
</panel>

### 74. DINH..LONG `@dinhcodes` (10 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/593" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Clarification on whether this counts as a feature flaw**
</div>

Hi Prof/Tutors/Friends,

Our team (**T15-1**) has adapted AB3 into a hall ledger. For most of our commands, users are expected to type in the full Student ID (case-insensitive, exact match) to execute commands, rather than using a list index.

**Examples:**
* `delete i=A1234567X`
* `edit i=A1234567X n=NAME ...`
* `tag i=A1234567X g=he y=Y2 m=Computer Science`

We believe that using the Student ID for commands is a better option than using an index for the following reasons:
* The ID is permanent and uniquely tied to the student, and does not change based on list ordering.
* Using ID reduces the likelihood of modifying the wrong contact by mistake.
* Given our product is designed for Resident Assistants managing student residents, we assume they are fast typists and will benefit from the precision of using official Student IDs.

However, we are also mindful of the rule stating:
> *"Unnecessarily complicated (or hard-to-type) command formats can be considered a `type.FeatureFlaw` as it is expected that the input formats will be optimized to get things done fast."*

We would like to seek your clarification on whether our design choice would be considered a feature flaw under this rule, so our team can prepare accordingly for the alpha debugging session next week. 

Thank you!
</panel>

<panel type="info"  collapsed>
<div slot="header">

### 75. QIAN..OWEN `@NuowenQ` (10 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/531" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: How to write test for GUI**
</div>

I am getting a 0% patch coverage error from Codecov on my UI tests.

Here is the error:
<img width="1770" height="1151" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e084256c-6487-4fe6-acbf-1db401526bd0" />

The fix which I have tried: 
'''java
package seedu.address.ui;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertDoesNotThrow;
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertFalse;
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assumptions.assumeTrue;

import java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch;

import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import javafx.application.Platform;
import javafx.stage.Stage;

/**
 * Contains tests for ``{``@code HelpWindow``}``.
 */
public class HelpWindowTest ``{``

    private static boolean toolkitAvailable;

    @BeforeAll
    public static void initToolkit() throws InterruptedException ``{``
        CountDownLatch latch = new CountDownLatch(1);
        try ``{``
            Platform.startup(latch::countDown);
            toolkitAvailable = true;
        ``}`` catch (IllegalStateException e) ``{``
            toolkitAvailable = true;
            latch.countDown();
        ``}`` catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) ``{``
            toolkitAvailable = false;
            latch.countDown();
        ``}``
        latch.await();
    ``}``

    @Test
    public void classInitializes() ``{``
        assertDoesNotThrow(() -&gt; Class.forName("seedu.address.ui.HelpWindow"));
    ``}``

    @Test
    public void constructorSuccess() throws Exception ``{``
        assumeTrue(toolkitAvailable, "JavaFX is not available in headless environment");
        CountDownLatch latch = new CountDownLatch(1);
        Platform.runLater(() -&gt; ``{``
            try ``{``
                HelpWindow helpWindow = new HelpWindow(new Stage());

                helpWindow.show();
                assertTrue(helpWindow.isShowing());

                helpWindow.focus();
                assertTrue(helpWindow.getRoot().isFocused());

                helpWindow.hide();
                assertFalse(helpWindow.isShowing());
            ``}`` finally ``{``
                latch.countDown();
            ``}``
        ``}``);

        latch.await();
    ``}``
``}``
'''
Issues:
1. Using assumeTrue(toolkitAvailable) — the test gets skipped entirely in CI, 
   giving 0% coverage.
2. Removing assumeTrue and using Platform.runLater with CountDownLatch — 
   the test fails in CI with an error because JavaFX has no display to render on.
3. Removing the UnsupportedOperationException catch so Platform.startup 
   is forced — still fails in CI headless environment.

The constructorSuccess test fails in CI because the GitHub Actions runner is a headless server with no display, so Platform.startup throws an UnsupportedOperationException, setting toolkitAvailable = false and causing assumeTrue to skip the test. Therefore I can't write a test for the GUI and if I don't do so there will be a coverage 0% error from codedov. May I know what should I do? 

My teammate solve a similar issue by :
- Moving GUI tests into a separate guitests package
- Excluding '**/GUITests/**' from the default test task so they don't run in CI
- Running them only via a separate testGui task with systemProperty 'ci', 'false'
<img width="1154" height="1280" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/310ae105-c1a3-4624-a58c-01216b557e7e" />

Can we ignore UI tests during CI and ignore the Codecov warning when merging the pull request, or is there a better approach to solve this issue?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/261" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: How to fix past commit messages without breaking history?**
</div>

## Environment
- OS: Windows 11

## Issue
The SE bot flagged two commit messages for having body lines that exceed 72 characters and I plan to fix them:
- `596d27b` - Use Java Streams in TaskList
- `53650be` - refactor: Reorganize project into package by feature structure

## Current Git History
'''
6e7aaa6 (tag: A-MoreErrorHandling) Improve error handling
...
...
596d27b Use Java Streams in TaskList
53650be (tag: A-FullCommitMessage) refactor: Reorganize project into package by feature structure
'''

To fix the commit messages, I plan to run:
'''bash
git rebase -i 53650be~1
# reword 53650be and 596d27b with fixed commit message
git push --force
'''

## Question
Since `53650be` is deep in history, this rebase will rewrite the hashes of all commits after it, including those with tags like `A-Assertions`, `A-CodeQuality`, `A-Streams`, etc.

Is it acceptable to proceed with `git push --force` in this case? Are there any concerns with the tags pointing to old hashes after the rebase?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/160#issuecomment-3858197778" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Hi, yes, you can close the GUI window programmatically when the user types "bye".

In your `chatbot.java` (equivalent chatbot class), add `Platform.exit()` to your bye method:
'''java
public String bye() ``{``
    this.storage.linesToFile(cqlist.getList());
    String message = "Bye. Hope to see you again soon!";
    Platform.exit();
    return ui.constructMessage(message);
``}``
'''

Make sure to import:
'''java
import javafx.application.Platform;
'''

`Platform.exit()` properly shuts down the JavaFX application and closes all windows.
</panel>

### 76. PHUA..ESTE `@select-e` (10 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/731" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: How to know if `response.NotInScope` is valid?**
</div>



The instructions say a bug can be considered `NotInScope` if 

> rectifying it is less important than the work that has been done already

As a tester, how are we supposed to determine if this is true? Do we just have to look at all of the product's features and give our best guess as to whether fixing the bug is more important than any of the implemented features?

The instructions seem to suggest we need to evaluate how much the effort of fixing it is "worth it". If we aren't acquainted with the implementation details, how would we know how much effort fixing the bug (e.g. adding a particular feature) would require?

Thanks!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/707" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2. :fas-info-circle: Clarification on the teaching teams decision for duplicate bugs**
</div>



I have two bug reports of the same bug, let's say one is the original (High severity) and the other is a duplicate (Medium severity).

If we were to downgrade the severity to Low and justify it in the original's comment, and both testers disagree, would the teaching team choose a severity out of all standpoints (i.e. the final severity will be either High, Medium or Low)?

Additionally, if only the tester of the original bug objects to the downgrade, would the teaching team only choose between High and Low severity?
</panel>

<div slot="header">

**6 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Thank you for your comments :) I have made changes to the format checking and user guide according to @junxiancheh's feedback.

**Changes:**
- Input is automatically trimmed when a message is sent.
- Blank messages cannot be sent.
- Commands are case-insensitive.
- Search keyword is also case-insensitive.
- A more specific response is returned when no tasks match a search keyword.
- Examples were added to the user guide.
</panel>

</div>

Hi guys!
Could you please help me test out my chatbot?

Link to JAR file: https://github.com/RumaisaKashif/ip/releases/tag/v0.3

Link to the User Guide: https://rumaisakashif.github.io/ip/

Which OS'es to test on: Windows | Mac | Linux
 
Things to check specifically:
- User Guide is consistent with the actual application behavior and commands.
- GUI loads correctly with no visual or functional bugs (feel free to suggest UX/UI improvements if needed).
- Window resizing works properly without layout breaking.
- Error handling works correctly for invalid inputs.
- Commands work as expected according to the User Guide.
- Saving and loading functionality works correctly.
- Data persists after closing and reopening the application.


Guide for testers:
* Post screenshots from your smoke tests
* Mention which OS you used for testing


</panel>

<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: New increment needed for A-Release?**
</div>

Hi, just wondering if I need to make a new increment with a tag (A-Release) and commit to my repo for the release even if there are no code changes between this and my latest commit?

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/387" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4. :fas-info-circle: GitHub Actions Java CI failing runtest.sh but works locally**
</div>

Hi all,

I have a project where everything works perfectly locally i.e. both compilation and tests pass. My GitHub Pages deployment also works fine.

However, when I run the Java CI workflow on GitHub Actions, I get this error:
```
Run ./runtest.sh
error: file not found: ../src/main/java/*.java
Usage: javac <options> <source files>
use --help for a list of possible options
********** BUILD FAILURE **********
```
Locally, running ./runtest.sh works fine.

I noticed that the script uses paths like ../src/main/java/*.java.

It seems the CI runner’s working directory is different, so the paths don’t exist there.

**Questions:**

1. Is this something I should worry about, since my Gradle build (./gradlew check) passes locally and my Pages deployment works?
2. What’s the best practice for handling such a CI issue? Should I fix the runtest.sh paths, or just rely on Gradle in CI?

Thanks for any clarification!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/541#issuecomment-4062200844" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**10 :fas-comment:**
</div>

If I recall correctly from CS2030S, LSP specifies that a subclass should not break the expectation of its superclass. Based on your arguments, it seems that ```Rectangle``` overrides ```resize``` from ```Square``` to allow resizing with different height and width. In this case, doesn't rectangle violates the expectation of its superclass despite ```Rectangle``` being less restrictive than ```Square``` regardless of the existence of another class calling ```Square.resize()```? 

From my perspective, I understand LSP based on expectation rather than restriction. I don't know whether this is the correct approach.
</panel>

</panel>

Hi @NUSOffisher, I have tested your chatbot.

### Working Components

<img width="400" height="627" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff4de48b-eed5-475e-b577-d0d924db870d" />

<img width="400" height="627" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f4b11317-8ec2-44f0-ad00-ed58fc7c49fd" />

All the task creations work perfectly when the user follows the appropriate format as per your user guide.

<img width="400" height="627" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/257cdbd2-2abb-4558-8d18-2e607a0db636" />

<img width="406" height="630" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95ebae35-f0d4-45e5-a175-934d5d119421" />

Other commands also seem to work well and I believe most of the functionality have been well handled by your chatbot. For instance when I try to find something that isnt there.
Also, after closing the program and rerunning my pre-added tasks do appear saved as I can access them again via list so your save and load feature works.

<img width="403" height="631" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3647488b-e850-4593-9295-86d3048f36b8" />

### Problems

<img width="402" height="628" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/31b84a4f-928e-4582-97d6-e013b9a07706" />

<img width="773" height="872" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63f63c29-bea4-4e1e-959e-5edbbdfd64f1" />

However there is a problem here. The parser does not validate the number of arguments before accessing array elements. As a result,  when the user uses less than ideal number of arguments, it causes an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException instead of graceful error handling. Proper input validation should be implemented to prevent this.

Other than that i really enjoyed your chatbot and thought it was interesting that you adopted a trivia extension! (first i have come across)
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/454#issuecomment-3945026562" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**7 :fas-comment:**
</div>

**OS used:** _Mac_

Hi @petelectron, :)

<img width="48%" height="627" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3820b168-58ea-42d0-a1a2-06558ae497be" />

<img width="48%" height="631" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8e1aab89-85d8-4e9a-bfea-540dc5d458af" />

### Task Creation Commands

I had no difficulty following the documented command formats to create tasks initially. The examples provided were clear for standard task creation.

However, I encountered some confusion regarding the “optional” field in the add command. The user guide labels the “do-after description” as optional, but it does not provide an example of a valid command where this field is omitted.

For example, I attempted:

`add todo, read book`

However, this did not work. Based on the formatting shown in the guide, it appears that the parser may still expect a second comma and some text right after, even if the optional field is omitted.

Because no example demonstrates how to correctly omit the optional field, it may not be entirely clear to users whether:
The field can truly be omitted entirely, or
A placeholder comma is still required.

**To improve clarity, you could:**

- Provide explicit examples showing both with and without the optional field.
- Clarify whether the comma must still be present when the optional field is omitted.
- Alternatively, restructure the syntax so the optional field is the last parameter, and allow parsing based on the number of commas provided.

From a design perspective, I also wondered whether the optional field is necessary for certain task types (such as `todo`). Users could potentially include additional contextual information directly in the description (e.g., “eat breakfast after brushing teeth”), which might reduce the need for a separate optional parameter.

<img width="48%" height="629" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e388e7c1-50b5-4867-b3c1-cfa1c6ae7653" />

<img width="48%" height="630" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9421d6ea-4bf3-4a65-824a-bdf5b209d98e" />

That said, I think your chatbot handles invalid inputs adequately.
Furthermore, the other commands seem to be working fine and tasks are saved upon restarting your chatbot.
nice aesthetics :) 
</panel>

Hi @microaugust,

I have tested all your commands and did not encounter any major issues. Overall, the chatbot functions reliably.

Your error handling is well implemented — especially for scenarios such as index out-of-bounds errors and invalid inputs (e.g., incorrect time formats). The feedback messages are clear and help guide the user appropriately.

One small suggestion would be to make the `find` command case insensitive. For example, in one of the screenshots, I tried `find homework`, but since the task was stored as `Homework`, the command did not return the expected result. Making the search case insensitive could improve usability and make the command more intuitive for users.

Other than that, the system works smoothly. Well done!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/475#issuecomment-3979734146" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**10 :fas-comment:**
</div>

### OS Used: MacOS

<img width="48%" height="631" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d9b1e6d9-ef0a-4396-88c7-1847ca2ea991" />

<img width="48%" height="628" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/feaefa5f-796e-4b7d-82c8-a9520550d622" />
-
<img width="48%" height="632" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfa5101c-2994-4cc4-8758-31fc63f77c2c" />

<img width="48%" height="630" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6de2bf29-c5f6-4bde-a024-89732e2a47e1" />
-
<img width="48%" height="633" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/919b4abe-895e-4370-a9d1-c32a648436ce" />

<img width="48%" height="631" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cf69b16e-3fca-4823-bb50-9e933ef5e95c" />

Hi @Ash-L7 ,

I’ve tested your chatbot and overall the commands work well. 
Error handling for cases such as invalid number of inputs, incorrect date/time formats, and index out-of-bounds are handled properly, which is great to see.

**One area that could be improved is the clarity of the error messages**. 
For example, when a user types an invalid command such as `deadline XXX` without the correct format, the error message shown is:

`Oops! Something went wrong: Text 'deadline' could not be parsed at index 0`

While this message is technically accurate (as it reflects the parsing exception), it may not be very intuitive for end users. Instead, you could consider displaying the correct command format, such as:

`Invalid format. Please use: deadline DESCRIPTION /by YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm`

This would allow users to immediately understand how to correct their input.
Additionally, you may want to avoid exposing low-level parsing messages directly to users, since they reflect internal implementation details rather than actionable guidance.
If your GUI supports it, you could also consider colour-coding error messages (e.g., using a bright red text style) so that users can immediately distinguish errors from normal chatbot responses. This would improve usability and make the interface more intuitive.

Overall, great job on the robustness of your error handling, just a few refinements to the user experience would make it even stronger.
</panel>

</panel>


<panel type="info"  collapsed>
<div slot="header">

### 81. HALE..HENG `@Haleycopter` (10 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/587" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Clarifications on feature flaws**
</div>



<img width="849" height="577" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/100d4502-0d40-4b34-b78b-3966e54e74bf" />

Our concern is balancing "Bugs related to duplicate detection" and "Overzealous input validation".
For example, we have restrictive rules for parameters `PHONE_NUMBER`. We are concerned if such restrictive rules would result in feature-flaw due to overzealous validation. But then again, if we lax our validation rules, this would break our intended duplicate detection between persons.

We are wondering if we could keep our existing input validation rules, as long as it is justifiable(aka laxxing the input validation rules would break the operations of our software)

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/194" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2. :fas-info-circle: Request to help with smoke testing JAR file**
</div>

* Link to JAR file: [here](https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/folders/1iCpSY2GbTx1w9ny1R0Ev0FKRGj2zeZPt) (might take a while >&lt;)
* Which OS'es to test on: Windows | Linux
* Things to check specifically:
- [ ] `event meeting /from 2026-01-02 /to 2026-01-01` (should return an error since start date is after end date)
- [ ] `event meeting /from 2026-01-02 /to 2026-01-02` (should be added to list)
- [ ] `list 2` (should return error saying nothing should come after list)
- [ ] ` mark 1` (should return error since there is additional space before `mark`)


Guide for testers:
* Post screenshots from your smoke tests
* Mention which OS you used for testing

Thank you!


</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/174#issuecomment-3866187671" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I think there are pros and cons for both options, and we can weigh them with some markers:

Maintainability
- Option 1: easy since only 1 class has to change exception handling logic
- Option 2: need update exception handling logic in all classes

Safety
- Option 1: developer must to remember to call exception handling class
- Option 2: very robust, nearly impossible to miss exception handling logic since each class has their own

Code clarity
- Option 1: very clean
- Option 2: quite messy

My 2 cents is that option 1 is optimal for managing internal logic while option 2 is optimal for high risk zones like user input 
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/587#issuecomment-4133589831" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**10 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Dear Prof Damith,

Our current duplicate handling rule touches `PHONE_NUMBER` and `EMAIL` param:
if params are not blank, duplicate if 2 person's `NAME` & `PHONE_NUMBER` & `EMAIL` are the equal

For `PHONE_NUMBER`, our input validation rules are:
STRING
if not blank:
(1) `+` followed `COUNTRY_CODE` and space
(2) followed by minimum 3 maximum 15 digits

For `EMAIL`, our input validation rules are taken from default AB3

We were wondering if laxing the validation rules by removing most of them is necessary to not cause overzealous input validation, in order to cater to unexpected anticipations like the following:

"While your software allows only one phone number in input values, a user might want to input 1234 5678 (HP) 1111-3333 (Office) -- blocking that input might not add any value but allowing it does."

But then again, laxing our validation rules to cater to unanticipated user inputs would spill over problems to duplicate handling rules.

So we were wondering if we could resolve this dilemma(or this dilemma doesn't even exist for us) if we:
1) specify in UG that we expect certain inputs and users should follow input validation rules
2) and if the user cannot attempt to use the missing feature or when the user does so, the software fails gracefully, possibly with a suitable error message i.e., the software should not crash (what our app already handles, which is proof that a user-expected feature is not in scope)
</panel>

</panel>

I think having the lambda expression span multiple lines kind of defeats the purpose of using such a simple lambda expression.

The Separator wrap check seems to be assuming that the "(" is the left bracket in the outermost function call, rather than what it is (a lambda as a parameter). If it were me, I would suppress both warnings.

'''

It works on Windows now 👍 

## 1. Launch and Commands
<img width="620" height="785" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6cbcc657-9e81-445c-ab38-b65c214bee94" />

Launched and commands work as intended. Small note - When find keyword has no results, the chatbot still says "here are your tasks", and when list is empty, there is no message at all.

<img width="624" height="784" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7cc18325-f6c2-48c3-b9b4-8b630c977cfd" />

<img width="603" height="277" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c5bb28d-e489-4e1b-b1e8-2fc2feea31ce" />

## 2. Error and User help

Error messages are specific and helpful to the commands invoking them, but maybe could also include the date/time format in error messages too.

<img width="592" height="792" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ab972fae-8d5e-457b-b804-3e69d0ac5f69" />

## 3. Data and Exit
Data carries over, and when save file is corrupted with garbage, handles it gracefully by overriding the data. Exit works as intended, with time afforded for user to read the goodbye message.

Great work overall and clean interface.
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/554#issuecomment-4103759059" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**8 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I had a similar class, also attempting to support multiple formats. What i did was to have a static final (constant) list of DateTimeFormatters, with the parse code trying each formatter on a loop. That way the bulk of hardcoding the formats were in the declarations rather than the methods.

Here is my code, for reference. Hope it is relevant to you!
'''c
private static final List&lt;DateTimeFormatter> DATE_TIME_FORMATTERS = List.of(
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd-MM-yyyy HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy/MM/dd HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd/MM/yyyy HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd-HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd/MM/yy HHmm"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd-MM-yy HHmm")
    );

    private static final List&lt;DateTimeFormatter> DATE_FORMATTERS = List.of(
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy-MM-dd"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd-MM-yyyy"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("yyyy/MM/dd"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd/MM/yyyy"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd/MM/yy"),
            DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd-MM-yy")

    );

public static Temporal parse(String input) throws IllegalValueException``{``
        input = input.trim();
        for (DateTimeFormatter formatter : DATE_TIME_FORMATTERS) ``{``
            try ``{``
                return LocalDateTime.parse(input, formatter);
            ``}`` catch (DateTimeParseException e) ``{``
                //ignore since exception is thrown below
            ``}``
        ``}``

        for (DateTimeFormatter formatter : DATE_FORMATTERS) ``{``
            try ``{``
                return LocalDate.parse(input, formatter);
            ``}`` catch (DateTimeParseException e) ``{``
                //ignore since exception is thrown below
            ``}``
        ``}``
        throw new IllegalValueException("Not a valid date/time: " + input);
    ``}``
'''
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/576#issuecomment-4118618683" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**9 :fas-comment:**
</div>

While defensive coding checks are important in large scale projects, in this tP I would prioritize potential failures from the user input side before misuse from the developer side if time limit is a concern, since your team is quite small, with errors from your teammates more easily rectified during code reviews and merging.

Following that I think it depends on the recoverability of the system? Such as Null pointer errors that might cause a crash (unrecoverable) vs adding a date like 31 Feb that doesnt exist (not intended, but not app breaking)
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/600#issuecomment-4171422339" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**10 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I think it was useful in my teams tP. In AB3 as an example, when you update the list of contacts, the PersonListPanel class sees it directly and handles the change, rebuilding the list elements after observing an update. My team had another panel that also would need to refresh when the same list was updated. 

Without Observer pattern, I would assume the List object would inform the Model , which would then in turn have to call the refresh methods in each UI class that needs to change. I could imagine how it can get way more complicated with many observers and observables, as the Model has to maintain a list of who to refresh based on which data structure's updates. I think this may then also violate the single-use principle, or even general abstraction, because this job of managing links should not be the job of unrelated higher-level processes.
</panel>

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/446" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: Errors after adding gradle to project**
</div>

OS: Mac M1 (silicon)
IDE: VS Code

Hi,
When I first added Gradle to my project several weeks ago, I followed the "Not Using an IDE" option as part of the [Gradle tutorial](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/gradle.html) (Scenario 2) because there was no VS code option then.

Firstly, is it ok for me to keep this or do I need to redo this part following the VS code guide? I tried doing so but encountered a couple errors so I would prefer to skip this step if possible.

Secondly, I thought I had finished the Gradle step as my Gradle CLI commands work perfectly fine (clear, test, build, run). However, I opened my project yesterday and discovered new errors:
- Several of my files had yellow underlined warnings at the top of the file stating: XXX.java is a non-project file
- The bottom of my VS code showed Java: Error and Gradle: Build Error

I can still run my Gradle CLI commands.

I spent many hours trying to debug this, also referring to issue #140 and #126
Here are all the failed approaches I've tried:
**- Reopening my project, making sure it's at the correct project root**
**- Checking I have Extension Pack for Java installed, including Gradle for Java**
**- Checking that my default Java version is Zulu17.56+15-CA**
**- Checking Workspace Trust to make sure my project is trusted**

**- Running the following commands in VS code command palette:**
_Java: Clean Java Language Server Workspace_
This didn't work so Chatgpt suggested I delete VS Code’s Java workspace cache for this project by renaming '''.vscode/''' to '''.vscode.bak/''' --> opening the VS code Java Language Server Log File to find '''../jdt_ws/...''' and deleting that folder. This still didn't work.

_Java: Configure Java Runtime_
Project Settings wouldn't open because my project wasn't recognised as a Java project. I could only open this after I fixed my issue (see the solution section)

**- Adding the following code to Preferences: Open User Settings (JSON) --> settings.json**
'''json
``{``
  "java.import.gradle.enabled": true,
  "gradle.buildServer.enabled": false,
  "java.jdt.ls.java.home": "/Users/sarinake/.sdkman/candidates/java/17.0.14.fx-zulu/zulu-17.jdk/Contents/Home",
  "java.configuration.runtimes": [
      "name": "JavaSE-17",
      "path": "/Users/sarinake/.sdkman/candidates/java/17.0.14.fx-zulu/zulu-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
  ]
``}``
'''
The command Preferences: Open User Settings (JSON) initially didn't work for me because of a broken symlink, so I had to do a bunch of reconfiguring before finally creating a new settings.json in '''Users/sarinake/Library/Application Support/Code/User/settings.json'''

This still didn't fix the issue, even though I tried multiple paths in settings.json:
- The java version recommended for Mac: '''/Users/sarinake/.sdkman/candidates/java/17.0.14.fx-zulu/zulu-17.jdk/Contents/Home'''
- Temurin 17 because gpt said it would work as well: '''/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-17.jdk/Contents/Home'''
- Termurin 21 because gpt thought there might be a Java version mismatch: '''/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-21.jdk/Contents/Home'''

**THE SOLUTION I FOUND**
Ultimately, I believe we found the issue in the Output log: '''Unsupported class file major version 65'''
- Major version 65 = Java 21.
- According to chatgpt, this meant VS Code’s Java/Gradle integration is trying to load a component (very likely the Gradle Build Server / importer from Microsoft’s Gradle tooling) that was compiled for Java 21
- But your Java language server process is running on Java 17 (Zulu/Temurin), so it cannot load Java 21 class files → initialization fails.

Chatgpt recommended I delete the poisoned Gradle init-script cache and disable the Gradle Build Server importer in '''ip/.vscode/settings.json''' by adding the following code:
'''json
``{``
  "gradle.buildServer.enabled": false,
  "java.import.gradle.enabled": true
``}``
'''

This didn't work so in the end, I used the most extreme method: Hard-disable the Gradle Build Server extension path by disabling the Gradle for Java extension.

My issues are finally resolved, but I'm worried this will cause issues later down the line. Is there another way I can solve this?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/247" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4. :fas-info-circle: Issue opening AB3 on mac silicon**
</div>

OS: Mac M1 silicon
IDE: VS Code

Hi, I am referring to the [Week 3 instructions](https://nus-cs2103-ay2526-s2.github.io/website/schedule/week3/project.html#1-individual-task-explore-ab3-features-sat-jan-31st-23-59)

I am having trouble opening the AB3 addressbook.jar file by running '''java java -jar addressbook.jar'''
Originally I was able to open it on my Windows home computer but now I wish to open it on my Mac PC (M1 silicon)

When I asked chatgpt, it explained that there was a JavaFX native-library architecture mismatch:
- Java is running as arm64 (need 'arm64')
- but the JavaFX native libs being loaded from ~/.openjfx/cache/... are x86_64 (have 'x86_64')

It recommended two solutions:
- Option A (fastest): run AB3 under Rosetta with an x86_64 JDK 17
- Option B (proper, arm64-native): change Gradle to use mac-aarch64 JavaFX artifacts

Which solution (or a 3rd option) is better for working on AB3 over the course of the entire semester?
</panel>

### 85. KOH ..YLAN `@RaisinMoldyBread` (9 posts) 
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/57#issuecomment-3794180572" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Windows Operating systems use backslash, but in human typing, we use a forward slash. I don't think it is a typo since Windows OS usually understands or converts to backslash. Usually, when you specify a forward slash directory, it is converted to a backslash in display, so either convention is technically not wrong

Additionally, for Unix/Linux uses forward slash, I think the instruction is assuming us as users to be in Windows, which might be why they used backslash
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/162#issuecomment-3858760395" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I think it is sort of known that Constructors should follow the naming convention of the Class. Meaning that the constructor should also be PascalCase. You can also see that the checkstyle script you run with Gradle also allows your constructor to be in PascalCase
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/577#issuecomment-4122884440" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**9 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I’d lean toward **Option 1 (embedding `List&lt;WorkoutLog>` inside `Person`)** because it aligns better with AB3’s existing design.

In AB3, `JsonSerializableAddressBook` → `JsonAdaptedPerson` → domain `Person` is already a well-established conversion pipeline. Extending `JsonAdaptedPerson` to include a `List&lt;JsonAdaptedWorkoutLog>` keeps everything consistent with how fields like tags are handled.

### Option 2: Separate JSON File

While storing logs in a separate JSON file may seem cleaner, it introduces additional complexity:

- You need a way to **link logs to persons** 
- Risk of **data inconsistency** (e.g. person deleted but logs remain)
- More complex **load/save sequencing and error handling**
- Less user-friendly if users manually edit storage files

This approach is only worth considering if:
- logs are very large, or
- you need independent querying/scaling in the future


</panel>

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/116#issuecomment-3828206014" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I'd use 'isTransactionSaved()' for something I expect a T/F for, and consider 'tryTransactionSave()' for something  I might want to try catch on. But honestly, if i want to do that it probably signals a possible mutation/error so 'useTransaction()' alone would have been best.

But just for sake of clarity since I'd think most people agree that isX sounds the most boolean, it might just be better to stick with that.
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/154#issuecomment-3906224060" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I'd say it works for now with this being a small project, but making `Ui` and `Storage` instance-based would be better in the long run, so it's good to note for your future projects.

That's because `Ui.promptInput()` or `Storage.storeTasks()` will be more difficult to test than just creating an instance and testing it directly. More troublesome with it being static, basically. 

Also, if you need multiple configurations or instances, you only have one static `Scanner` in your `Ui` class, so you can't have different setups. For example, you can't easily test with mock input or have different storage paths for different contexts. (Fine for iP though, since it's a simple single-user app!)

Having instance methods allows you to:
```
Ui ui = new Ui();
Storage storage = new Storage("./data/duke.txt");

// Now you can:
// - Mock ui/storage in tests
// - Have multiple instances with different configs
```
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/539#issuecomment-4060668268" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**8 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Branch `Y` will contain all the commits from `branch-X` plus the new commits you added on top.

When `branch-X` is merged into `master`, those commits are already part of `master`. Since `branch-Y` was created from `branch-X`, it also contains those same commits, so when you later try to merge `branch-Y` into `master`, Git will recognise that the commits from `branch-X` are already present in `master`, so only the new commits unique to `branch-Y` will be merged.

If your fork’s `master` is behind the upstream `master`, you can just resync it and then rebase `branch-Y` onto the updated `master`. This helps keep your commit history cleaner (more linear - see GitTree visualisation to see what i mean), although simply merging also works if there are no conflicts.

Hope this helps :)
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/547#issuecomment-4067128980" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**9 :fas-comment:**
</div>

I think it should generally be fine as long as the stream chain remains reasonable readable, like your example.

Personally, i'd only consider splitting if the stream chain becomes longer or if each step contains more complex logic, e.g. if the filter or map involves multiple conditions, during which it might be clearer(?) to extract them into helpers instead of keeping everything within a chain :)
</panel>

</panel>

I tested it on my own windows computer by downloading JavaFX 17 from https://gluonhq.com/products/javafx/ on it, as I did not use this windows computer to code for this module and followed up with the code `java --module-path "C:\Path\To\javaFX\javafx-sdk-17.0.18\lib" --add-modules javafx.controls,javafx.fxml -jar Pompompurin.jar` and successfully ran the program.

'''java

'''

**1. :fas-info-circle: Multiplicity of set of enumeration class**
</div>

If Class A has a set of enumeration class B as 1 attribute, should the multiplicity of class B be the number of values available in B ?

For example,
```
class A ``{``
    Set<B> slots;
``}``

public enum B ``{``
    SLOT_1,
    SLOT_2,
    ...,
    SLOT_12
``}``
```
Then should the multiplicity be as follows:

<img width="120" height="156" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/480d116b-ecd3-4618-83f1-cd8ef5536357" />

<div slot="header">

**4 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Yes you can! For me, I added a method to Duke.java to return the exit status of a command. Then I edited the `handleUserInput` method to check if the command indicates an exit. I also added a pause transition so the farewell message shows as follows:
```
Stage stage = (Stage) dialogContainer.getScene().getWindow();
PauseTransition pause = new PauseTransition(Duration.seconds(1));
pause.setOnFinished(e -&gt; ``{``
     stage.close();
``}``);
pause.play();
```
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/255#issuecomment-3920365412" expanded>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/208" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: [iP] Seeking clarification on detached commit warning for Gradle-related commits**
</div>

Hi everyone, while double-checking my project setup to ensure I haven't missed any technical steps, I noticed that my repository displays a warning for Gradle-related commits (e.g., "Add Gradle support", "Bump gradle and lib version", etc.): "This commit does not belong to any branch on this repository." I am concerned that I might have inadvertently missed a step or performed the merge incorrectly. I wanted to clarify if this status indicates a mistake in my setup that needs to be fixed, or if it is an acceptable state for the repository at this stage.


</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/208#issuecomment-3904534714" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**4 :fas-comment:**
</div>

No Prof, everything is there and is up-to-date on both progress and code dashboard. 
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/208#issuecomment-3904733131" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**5 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Thank you, Prof.

I recalled and checked my reflog and realised that I used git pull --rebase while integrating the Gradle-related commits. I see now in the course guidelines that rebasing is discouraged as it changes the timestamps of commits. 
The warning appears on the top of the screen specifically when I click those commits. However, I did not perform any force push or intentionally rewrite the remote history. Since the dashboards are currently passing, please let me know if any further action is required to rectify this, or if I should leave it as is. Thank you.
</panel>

**1. :fas-info-circle: Code Quality: Unavoidable long method**
</div>

I was notified that my iP may have fallen short of the requirement on not having overly long methods. From the peer evaluation, the issue was flagged in my Parser class — I suspect it's my dateTimeParser method, where I tried to make it more adaptable by supporting 8 different valid date/time formats, which increased the length.

Would this be penalised given that the length comes from handling multiple format conditions? Or would it be simpler to just remove that adaptability?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/669#issuecomment-4367121531" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**9 :fas-comment:**
</div>

> Yeah you're right about the navigability arrows, I actually have them for the Handout-Brochure associations (same line) but I missed it for the Brochure self-association, good catch. For the * thing, I think using * is fine because it just means unconstrained, for the cover relationship, the question only says each Brochure has exactly one Handout as cover, so we put 1 on the Handout side. But it doesn't say anything about how many Brochures can share the same Handout as cover, so * on the Brochure side just means we're not adding any restriction that wasn't stated. It's not really assuming it's many, more like saying there's nothing stopping it from being many. Leaving it blank is also fine though I think.
> 
> These are my updated diagrams: <img alt="Image" width="1280" height="752" src="https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/116509027/586746344-c64c0a80-f67d-4400-9b16-759f99cc191f.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3Nzc4NDA2MzIsIm5iZiI6MTc3Nzg0MDMzMiwicGF0aCI6Ii8xMTY1MDkwMjcvNTg2NzQ2MzQ0LWM2NGMwYTgwLWY2N2QtNDQwMC05YjE2LTc1OWY5OWNjMTkxZi5wbmc_.a6SVenZJFSOmhzEccOd9vYLDkecp2MHfdTZSDoEmjxM">

Great diagram! however I believe that the arrow directions of your association labels seem to be wrong as it should read towards the arrow, e.g. in the below diagram it reads as "Player plays SquareGame". 
Actually for your case, using association role might be a better choice (as you did not use verbs but nouns in your diagram).

<img width="592" height="420" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28a3b0bc-8874-4ef6-b78c-1b0425cca368" />
</panel>

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/455" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2. :fas-info-circle: 💡Tables not working in github.io markdown**
</div>

EDIT: The cause is not `<br>`. You need 2 blank lines above a table to properly render it.

Just sharing a tip for those of you using tables for the project website markdown!

The one in github.io can't have `<br>` in the table cells. Otherwise it'll break:

<img width="1261" height="531" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0843b6b-2163-42c3-93e2-f2a7ca7f93f5" />

Removing the `<br>` allows it to render properly:

<img width="1271" height="858" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75dee489-3b51-47be-aa48-eea9d1237eb0" />

I think this issue is caused by different processing between GFMD and normal markdown.
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/445" expanded>

UI looks good! However, I found a bug where the command cannot be sent if the date is formatted improperly (for deadline), and no error message is shown so the user might not know what went wrong. The bug happens when you already write `/by` but fail to provide a date in the correct format (even empty date).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fee82074-55d2-4faa-8c43-35abef481c75

Error message shown in the terminal:
```
Caused by: java.time.format.DateTimeParseException: Text '20-02-2026' could not be parsed at index 2
        at java.base/java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.parseResolved0(DateTimeFormatter.java:2108)
        at java.base/java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter.parse(DateTimeFormatter.java:2010)
        at java.base/java.time.LocalDateTime.parse(LocalDateTime.java:494)
        at woody.task.Deadline.<init>(Deadline.java:18)
        at woody.Woody.deadline(Woody.java:152)
        at woody.Woody.run(Woody.java:51)
        at woody.ui.MainWindow.handleUserInput(MainWindow.java:65)
```

Perhaps you can handle this exception and show an error message to the user with instructions on how to format the date properly?

### 93. KELV.. KIT `@kelv5` (9 posts) <span class=text-warning>:octicon-eye:</span>
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/728" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Clarification on severity when invalid command format is silently accepted**
</div>

Suppose a command accepts invalid command formats such as extra parameters unsupported, does not show any error or warning and executes exactly the same result as a valid command, 

would this be considered as Low (since functionality still works), or Medium (since it affects correctness of command validation)?

Also, does the severity change if:
- the command structure is relatively complex
- the system gives a misleading error message which does not match the actual issue at some situation



</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/705" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2. :fas-info-circle: Request permission to combine Similar Parser Validation Bugs**
</div>

From this [link](https://nus-cs2103-ay2526-s2.github.io/website/admin/tp-pe.html?cv-highlight=W3sidCI6IlAiLCJpIjo4LCJwIjoiYWRkaXRpb25hbEd1aWRlbGluZXNGb3JCdWdUcmlhZ2luZyIsInMiOiJXaGF0IGJ1Z3MgY2FuIGJlIGNvbnNpZGVyZWQgZHVwbCIsImgiOi02NjA1MzgxMH1d#pe-phase-2-developer-response), it is stated that the dev team may request permission to combine independently-fixable yet similar bugs into a single issue.

Currently, our team has received multiple bug reports that all come from same underlying issue of how invalid parameters were handled at the parser level. Across different commands, the behaviour is similar when invalid prefixes are supplied, the system shows incorrect error message. However, similar issues of incorrect error messages were reported separately in different bug reports after being wrapped under different commands.

Although reported separately under different commands, they are caused by the same cause in parser logic. From our side, these issues can be fixed in a similar way by introducing a shared common helper method at the parser level to detect invalid prefixes, and calling the same helper method consistently across all affected commands. This fix shows that they are multiple similar instances of the same underlying issue.

So, we would like to request to combine these reports into a single bug report as per the PE guidelines. Could you please advise on the appropriate process to obtain approval for this request?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/503" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3. :fas-info-circle: Typo in AB3 Tutorial 2: Adding a Command**
</div>

In AB3 Tutorial 2, under the section of [Updating Storage](https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/ab3AddRemark.html#updating-storage), there is a typo in `Updating tests to match....` as follows:

'''java
.withPhone("94351253")
.withPhone("94351253").withRemark("She likes aardvarks.")
'''
`withPhone("94351253")` is repeated twice

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/389#issuecomment-3935677776" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**7 :fas-comment:**
</div>

OS: Windows 11

The app launches successfully and I've tested all the commands supported and almost all working as excepted. Below are my findings:

- I really like the weather theme and the overall UI experience. Especially, the ability to scroll through command history using the arrow keys is a great addition to improve the user experience.

- Almost all invalid inputs are handled well with a different styling for error messages and clear explanation shown.

- Currently, the user guide states that "the date format must be `yyyy-mm-dd HHmm`". However,  I noticed that tasks could still be added without time as below. You might want to consider updating user guide or modifying the implementation for consistency.

<img width="832" height="454" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe337d4c-9772-4b55-bd2a-ffb69b0b9ac7" />

- I'm not sure if this is the intended behaviour, but when user tries to update a task without providing any updated values, the chatbot message indicates that task details have been updated, although no changes were actually made as below. Perhaps you could try to improve the response message for clarity.

<img width="778" height="836" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29e140a6-d496-46d8-b711-3364bd6955d5" />

- I also like how users are able to convert a todo task into deadline task with a single update command by supplying `/by <date>`

- Data persistence works as expected after starting another session as below.

<img width="795" height="794" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c4e471b-a517-4c40-b610-b02bb3b1b046" />

- Duplicated tasks and invalid dates such as `2025-02-31` are detected correctly and handled well.
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/422#issuecomment-3938855084" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**8 :fas-comment:**
</div>

OS: Windows 11

Overall, the application launches successfully and all commands work well as intended according to the user guide. 

Invalid inputs are mostly covered and handled gracefully, and data persistence works well as the tasks are recovered after restarting the application as followed.

<img width="838" height="835" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b6b5ce01-6c07-48b9-8ad8-4a2ae0d655f5" />

Here are some additional edge cases of invalid user input you might consider handling:

- May want to handle invalid dates such as `2026-02-31` as they were accepted instead of flagged as invalid currently as below.

<img width="798" height="494" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b9435e3-36cd-4715-b781-a06d6302eb4d" />

- Might consider handling invalid cases where start date/time is later than end date/time.

Generally, great job with core functionality working well and smooth user experience.

### 94. TIAN..ARUI `@Tian-Jiarui` (9 posts) <span class=text-warning>:octicon-eye:</span>
</div>


<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/354" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Request to help with smoke testing JAR file**
</div>

## Smoke test request: Valencia (CS2103 iP)

**Update:** I re-uploaded a new release (**v0.3**) after fixing/improving a feature. Please use **v0.3** for testing (ignore v0.2).

**Link to JAR file:** [JAR Download Link](https://github.com/Tian-Jiarui/ip/releases/download/v0.3/valencia.jar)
**Link to the User Guide:** [User Guide](https://tian-jiarui.github.io/ip/)
**Link to the valencia_v0.3:** [v0.3](https://github.com/Tian-Jiarui/ip/releases/tag/v0.3)
**Which OS’es to test on:** Windows | Linux  (I am using MAC :D!, feel free to test even if you are MAC though!)

---

## Guide for testers
Please:
- Post a screenshot of the app running
- Mention your OS (Windows/Mac/Linux + version if possible)
- Note any crashes / weird behaviour

---

## Things to check

### 1) Commands work as described in the User Guide
- Greetings: `hi` / `hello` / `hey`
- Add tasks:
  - `todo &lt;desc>`
  - `deadline &lt;desc> /by yyyy-MM-dd`
  - `event &lt;desc> /from &lt;start> /to &lt;end>`
- View / search:
  - `list`
  - `find &lt;keyword>`
  - `remind [days]`
- Modify tasks:
  - `mark &lt;task number>` / `unmark &lt;task number>`
  - `delete &lt;task number>`
- Easter egg:
  - `pickup` / `pickupline` / `pickup-line` / `pickup line` / `pick up line`
- Exit:
  - `bye`

### 2) Error handling
- Invalid inputs (e.g., missing task number, wrong date format, out-of-range index)

### 3) Data persistence
- Add a few tasks → close app → reopen → tasks + done status should still be there

### 4) UI behaviour
- Scrolling, Send button, pressing Enter to send, resizing window (if applicable)

---

Thanks a lot!! 🙏

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/350" expanded>

Tested on: **Mac** (MacBook M3 Pro)
Java: `java -version` → **Azul Zulu 17**

### ✅ Launch
- App launches successfully.

<img width="592" height="37" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dd35a465-cc44-43f7-a925-76bc4165a6f4" />
<img width="417" height="629" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ae0cd2b-eb9c-4572-b864-17742c710008" />

### ✅ Core commands
- `list`, `todo`, `deadline`, `event` work as described in the UG.
- `mark` / `unmark` updates status correctly.
- `find report` returns matching task(s).

<img width="413" height="627" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad93fa7c-5f32-4e46-b168-151424509d56" />
<img width="419" height="630" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d43aa95b-a856-47fe-bf79-01b35d3b6b7f" />

### ✅ Date formatting
- Valid: `deadline submit report /by 2026-02-20` works.
- Invalid: `deadline bad date /by 20-02-2026` correctly rejects and asks for `YYYY-MM-DD`.

<img width="423" height="252" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/35850b55-fbd1-469f-80bd-c81a2d2f05e0" />

### ✅ Exit
- `bye` closes as expected.

No crashes/weird behaviour encountered during normal use. 👍 The interface is really cute and the color matches the pokemon theme well! the bye closing the app is really cool and is something that I didnt even plan for my own chatbot! good touch man :)

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/117#issuecomment-3830411699" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**2 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Hi @LouisC-Yu I tried to understand what went wrong and the important information is that you did a force push. (A fix to a smaller problem but it causes some major aftereffects you're experiencing now)

My guess is that your fork (`LouisC-Yu/ip`) lost the original commit history due to a force push from an **unrelated** repo. You probably created a locally initialised repo (_See comment below_) instead of cloning the fork. When you force pushed, that new history replaced the fork’s original history.

As the two repository has no common ancestor now, GitHub is confused as to how to "merge" (something like: at which point in the repo's histories should I start merging from?), so it can’t create a PR.

I am thinking you can fix it by reapplying your files (with the edits you have done on this `LouisC-Yu/ip`, unrelated repo) on top of upstream’s `master` so your repo becomes a **child** of the original history again.

Here's a set of possible solution:

 1. Save everything first (so that we won't accidentally force overwrite anything; Really impt in case my recommendation fails, you can still save this copy)
```
git add .
git commit -m "backup before fixing history"
git branch backup-broken
```

2. Add the real ip repo as upstream
```
git remote add upstream https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/ip.git
git fetch upstream
```

3. Create a new branch that starts from the real history
```
git checkout -b fixed-master upstream/master
```

Now this branch has correct ip commit history.

4. Bring Louis’s project files onto this branch
```
git checkout master -- .
```

(This copies his current files into this branch.)

5. Commit his work on top of upstream
```
git add .
git commit -m "Apply my project changes on top of upstream master"
```
Now history becomes:

ip commits → Louis commit (Imagine this a nodes of a graph, your commit now builds on the ip commits)

6. Push the fixed branch
```
git push origin fixed-master
```

7. Create PR

On GitHub:
```
base: NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/ip → master
compare: Louis repo → fixed-master
```
PR will now work because there is a common ancestor.

_Comment: You did either of the following.
(1) Zipped the repo and download, so you only have the files but not shared history. Something like having `x1 = [1], x2 = [1]` but `x1 is x2 == False` in python, simply 2 different objects;
(2) Ran `git init` instead of cloning the fork properly._ 

I used ChatGPT to help reason about the Git history issue and to get directions about the steps to take to allow a PR to be created. I read and adapted the explanation and commands before posting. Prompt (instructions quoted from @damithc 's instructions):
"Louis has an issue creating a PR from a forked repo to the upstream repo. Come up with a concrete set of steps/commands to go from the current 'broken' state to a state from which the PR can be created. Currently, the problem is there are no common commits between the upstream repo https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/ip.git and the local repo. Recommend for me the sequence of commands Louis should run"

Feel free to comment, thanks!
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/117#issuecomment-3830677897" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**3 :fas-comment:**
</div>

> [@henghengyh](https://github.com/henghengyh) Would this also preserve [@LouisC-Yu](https://github.com/LouisC-Yu)'s tags, so that our grading scripts can still count the increments as done?

Hmmm, I was wrong, this will create brand new commits which do not preserve the tags. From what I see there are two options: (1) move the tags over to the new commits, or (2) recreate the tags manually. This may lose record of the actual commit timestamp though. 
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/302#issuecomment-3931252979" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**6 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Thanks @Lim-ZY An Update: I have now updated my fat JAR File. To anyone else who might be facing the same issue, here's the reason why my JAR File (~8MB) kept failing. 

I added an additional, unnecessary, error-causing chunk at the bottom of my `build.gradle` file:
```
javafx ``{``
    version = "17"
    modules = [ 'javafx.controls', 'javafx.fxml' ]
``}``
```

This resulted in not all JavaFX Dependencies being exported.

Thus, I have updated my forum post above to `v2.4` which reflects the latest change and has a JAR File (~10MB)
</panel>

d36f224 (HEAD -&gt; main) shapes.txt: Add some shapes

69e4341 Update fruits list

d36f224 (HEAD -&gt; main) shapes.txt: Add some shapes

aef9f25 Add elderberries and figs into fruits.txt

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/395" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Organisation Name is Taken for TP**
</div>

## Issue: Team Organisation Name Already Taken

My team attempted to create our team organisation for the TP using the name **`AY2526S2-CS2103-F08-1`**, but GitHub indicated that the organisation name had already been taken.

To allow our team to proceed with the repository setup and other preparations, we have temporarily renamed our organisation to **`AY2526S2-CS2103-F08-01`**.

Because of this, our PR was closed due to the fact that it did not comply to the allowed names and also our TP progress is not updated on the dashboard.

May we check if there is a way to identify who has taken our intended organisation name, or if there is any recommended action we should take?
</panel>

# Smoke Test Report

## Test Environment
- **OS tested on:** macOS
- **Java version:** openjdk 17.0.14 2025-01-21 LTS

## Launch Test
App launches successfully using `java -jar mrducky.jar`

## Basic Command Checks
Tested the following commands and they worked as expected:

- `help`
- `list`
- `todo`
- `deadline`
- `event`
- `mark` / `unmark`
- `find`
- `delete`
- `bye`

### Commands Ran
```
help
list
todo go home
deadline do assignment /by 03/03/2026 1800
event cny gathering /from 19/02/2026 1400 /to 19/02/2026 2030
deadline /by 03/03/2026 1800
mark 2
unmark 2
find cny
delete 1
bye
```

## Data Storage
Data file is created in the local folder with no issues

## Error Handling
App does not crash on invalid index (e.g., `mark 99`)  
App handles wrong date format gracefully with an error message
App handles any invaid commands properly

## Notes / Issues
None observed
A minor comment is that the bye command does not shut down the app

</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/386#issuecomment-3933931926" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**6 :fas-comment:**
</div>

To fix, follow the "Setting up Java FX" part in this guide: [https://se-education.org/guides/tutorials/javaFxPart1.html](url)

I saw your build.gradle and you are just lacking this part in your dependencies.

```
    String javaFxVersion = '17.0.7'

    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-base', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'win'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-base', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'mac'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-base', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'linux'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-controls', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'win'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-controls', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'mac'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-controls', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'linux'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-fxml', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'win'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-fxml', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'mac'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-fxml', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'linux'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-graphics', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'win'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-graphics', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'mac'
    implementation group: 'org.openjfx', name: 'javafx-graphics', version: javaFxVersion, classifier: 'linux'
```
</panel>

**1. :fas-info-circle: Clarification on whether the benefit clause in user stories is required**
</div>

While reviewing the materials on user story format and grading guidelines, I noticed a potential inconsistency regarding the benefit clause in user stories.

As stated in the grading guide:

<img width="374" height="152" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7bebfdd3-f892-4f6c-8b19-9789f5c6aae5" />

This seems to imply that all three components of the user story format should be present:
As a ``{``role``}``, I can ``{``function``}``, so that ``{``benefit``}``.

However, another material states:

<img width="859" height="237" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7b237fd8-afd3-42a3-a2be-4de853265eba" />

Could we clarify whether user stories without the benefit clause should still be considered valid if the benefit is obvious?
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/153#issuecomment-3852788558" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**5 :fas-comment:**
</div>

To add on to the above answers, one way to keep things cleaner is to let the logic layer signal that an exit is required, and let the UI decide how to close the window.

For example, my duke (neko) has an exit flag (e.g. hasExited()), which the UI checks after handling user input.

Rough sketch of my ```handleUserInput```:
```
private void handleUserInput() ``{``
    String input = userInput.getText();

    dialogContainer.getChildren().addAll(
            DialogBox.getUserDialog(input, userImage),
            DialogBox.getNekoDialog(neko.run(input), nekoImage)
    );
    userInput.clear();

    if (neko.hasExited()) ``{``
        closeWindowWithDelay(3.0);
    ``}``
``}``
```
It is a suggestion but highly depends on how your code structure works.
</panel>

<div slot="header">

**1. :fas-info-circle: Request to help with smoke testing JAR file**
</div>

* Link to JAR file: https://github.com/SpaceMongoose/ip/releases/download/A-SmokeTest/esquie.jar
* Link to the User Guide: https://spacemongoose.github.io/ip/

* Things to check specifically:

Guide for testers:

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/615#issuecomment-4194665859" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**7 :fas-comment:**
</div>

To my understanding, since sets in Java can only contain unique elements, I think that the upper bound multiplicity would be equal to the number of values available in B as you mentioned.

The lower bound could either be 0 or 1, depending on if we allow slots to be empty or not. 🤔 
</panel>

<panel  popup-url="https://github.com/NUS-CS2103-AY2526-S2/forum/issues/646#issuecomment-4247497409" expanded>
<div slot="header">

**8 :fas-comment:**
</div>

Yes, this is considered a bug. As noted in previous comments, bugs inherited from AB3 are still counted. Furthermore, since these characters are used in real-world names, AB3's original validation was overly restrictive, which inherently makes it a bug.
</panel>

</panel>
